message:
licensing: adopt MIT + OpenAI/Anthropic rider across workspace

Summary
- Add top-level LICENSE file containing the full MIT license text with the
  OpenAI/Anthropic rider language.
- Align root workspace metadata to file-based licensing by replacing
  `license = "MIT"` with `license-file = "LICENSE"`.
- Align all member crate manifests to inherit workspace `license-file`
  metadata (`license-file.workspace = true`) instead of plain MIT string
  inheritance.
- Update README badge and License section text so repository docs and Cargo
  metadata describe the same licensing state.

Details
- README license badge now points to the rider form and links to LICENSE.
- README terminal License section now states:
  `MIT License (with OpenAI/Anthropic Rider). See LICENSE.`
- Workspace/package metadata is now coherent for crates.io/package consumers:
  every crate resolves licensing through the checked-in LICENSE file.

Rationale
- Prevent contradictory license signaling between docs, workspace metadata,
  crate manifests, and repository legal text.
- Ensure downstream tooling reads consistent package metadata after the rider
  migration.

Notes
- No behavioral/runtime code changes are included in this commit.

files.patch:
@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ members = [
 
 [workspace.package]
 edition = "2024"
-license = "MIT"
+license-file = "LICENSE"
 repository = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite"
 rust-version = "1.85"

@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@

+For purposes of this rider, "use" includes, without limitation: copying,

+This rider applies to the Software and all Derivative Works. As a condition of

+You agree that a breach of this rider would cause irreparable harm and that
